# Roost

> Roost is a Laravel-inspired full-stack framework for building applications on Cloudflare Workers. Convention-over-configuration for the edge — models, routing, auth, queues, AI agents, and more, all wired together out of the box.

@doc-version: 0.1.0

## Tutorials

- [Tutorials](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/tutorials/index.md): Learning-oriented lessons that guide you through building real features with Roost.
- [Deploy to Cloudflare](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/tutorials/deploy-to-cloudflare.md): Take your Roost application from local development to production on Cloudflare Workers.
- [Build a REST API](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/tutorials/build-a-task-api.md): Create a CRUD API with database models, validation, and tests.
- [Build a SaaS App](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/tutorials/build-a-saas-app.md): Add authentication, subscription billing, and background jobs to a Roost application.
- [Build an AI Chat App](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/tutorials/build-a-chat-app.md): Create a chat interface powered by Cloudflare Workers AI with conversation history.

## Guides

- [Roost Documentation](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/index.md): Auth, ORM, AI agents, job queues, billing, and more — on Cloudflare Workers with zero cold-start overhead.
- [Quick Start](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/getting-started.md): Install the CLI, create a project, and deploy your first Roost application.
- [@roostjs/queue Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/queue.md): Task-oriented instructions for defining jobs, dispatching, chaining, batching, and testing.
- [@roostjs/cli Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/cli.md): Task-oriented instructions for scaffolding, code generation, migrations, and deployment.
- [@roostjs/cloudflare Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/cloudflare.md): Task-oriented instructions for Cloudflare bindings: D1, R2, KV, Queues, Workers AI, and Vectorize.
- [@roostjs/events Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/events.md): Task-oriented instructions for defining events, registering listeners, using subscribers, deferring listeners to the queue, and testing dispatches.
- [Migrations](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/migrations.md): Task-oriented instructions for creating, running, and managing database schema changes.
- [@roostjs/feature-flags Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/feature-flags.md): Task-oriented instructions for defining flags in WorkOS, checking them in handlers, scoped user/org evaluation, prefetching with middleware, and testing flag-gated behavior.
- [How-to Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/index.md): Task-oriented instructions for accomplishing specific goals with Roost.
- [@roostjs/auth Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/auth.md): Task-oriented instructions for authentication, authorization, sessions, and multi-tenancy.
- [@roostjs/testing Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/testing.md): Task-oriented instructions for HTTP tests, response assertions, fakes, and database isolation.
- [@roostjs/start Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/start.md): Task-oriented instructions for routing, server functions, and SSR with TanStack Start.
- [Environment](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/environment.md): Task-oriented instructions for managing environment variables and secrets across local and production environments.
- [Error Handling](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/error-handling.md): Task-oriented instructions for handling errors in routes, jobs, and across the stack.
- [@roostjs/core Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/core.md): Task-oriented instructions for the container, config, middleware, and service providers.
- [@roostjs/broadcast Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/broadcast.md): Task-oriented instructions for setting up broadcasting, creating broadcastable events, connecting a client, authorizing private channels, and testing.
- [Deployment](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/deployment.md): Task-oriented instructions for deploying Roost applications to Cloudflare Workers.
- [@roostjs/workflow Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/workflow.md): Task-oriented instructions for defining workflows, running durable steps, implementing sagas with Compensable, and testing without Cloudflare.
- [@roostjs/ai Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/ai.md): Task-oriented instructions for agents, tools, streaming, and testing. Powered by Cloudflare Workers AI — no API keys required.
- [@roostjs/billing Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/billing.md): Task-oriented instructions for Stripe integration, subscriptions, webhooks, and metered billing.
- [@roostjs/orm Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/orm.md): Task-oriented instructions for models, querying, relationships, factories, and testing.
- [@roostjs/schema Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/schema.md): Task-oriented instructions for defining tool input schemas, optional fields, nested objects, and descriptions.
- [@roostjs/mcp Guides](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/guides/mcp.md): Task-oriented instructions for building MCP servers with tools, resources, and prompts.

## Reference

- [@roostjs/queue](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/queue.md): Background job processing on Cloudflare Queues. Typed job classes with retry configuration, dispatch methods, chaining, batching, and lifecycle hooks.
- [@roostjs/cli](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/cli.md): The roost command-line tool for project scaffolding, code generation, database operations, and deployment.
- [@roostjs/cloudflare](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/cloudflare.md): Typed wrappers around all Cloudflare Worker bindings: D1, KV, R2, Queues, AI, Vectorize, Durable Objects, and Hyperdrive.
- [@roostjs/events](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/events.md): In-process event dispatching with listener registration, subscriber classes, optional queue integration, and broadcast interop.
- [@roostjs/feature-flags](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/feature-flags.md): Feature flag evaluation backed by WorkOS with KV edge caching. FeatureFlag static API, scoped evaluation, per-request caching, middleware, providers, and service provider.
- [Reference](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/index.md): Technical descriptions of every package, class, and method in the Roost framework.
- [@roostjs/auth](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/auth.md): WorkOS-backed authentication with session management, middleware guards, CSRF protection, RBAC, and multi-tenancy support.
- [@roostjs/testing](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/testing.md): HTTP test client, response assertions, unified fakes, and test setup utilities for Roost applications on bun:test.
- [@roostjs/start](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/start.md): Bridges the Roost framework with TanStack Start. Provides the context bridge, middleware factory, and server function wrappers.
- [@roostjs/core](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/core.md): Dependency injection container, configuration management, middleware pipeline, application lifecycle, and service provider base class.
- [@roostjs/broadcast](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/broadcast.md): Real-time WebSocket broadcasting via Cloudflare Durable Objects. Channel types, ChannelDO, BroadcastManager, client helper, and testing utilities.
- [@roostjs/workflow](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/workflow.md): Durable multi-step workflows on Cloudflare Workflows. Workflow base class, WorkflowClient, Compensable, and service provider.
- [@roostjs/ai](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/ai.md): Class-based AI agents with typed tools, conversation memory, and streaming. Powered exclusively by Cloudflare Workers AI — no API keys required.
- [@roostjs/billing](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/billing.md): Abstract billing provider interface with a Stripe adapter. Implemented using only fetch for Cloudflare Worker compatibility — no Node.js SDK.
- [@roostjs/orm](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/orm.md): Laravel-inspired ORM built on Drizzle for D1 databases. Model classes, query builder, relationships, lifecycle hooks, factories.
- [@roostjs/schema](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/schema.md): Fluent JSON Schema builder with zero runtime dependencies. Used by @roostjs/ai and @roostjs/mcp to define tool and prompt parameters.
- [@roostjs/mcp](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/reference/mcp.md): Model Context Protocol server implementation. Expose application capabilities as tools, resources, and prompts to MCP-compatible AI clients.

## Optional

- [@roostjs/queue](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/queue.md): How Cloudflare Queues work, the job lifecycle from dispatch to completion, retry strategies, and why queues beat cron for deferred work.
- [Service Container](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/service-container.md): How Roost manages dependencies, why dependency injection matters at the edge, and how service providers wire everything together.
- [@roostjs/cli](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/cli.md): The code generation philosophy behind Roost's generators, how scaffolding reduces decision fatigue, and convention enforcement through generators.
- [@roostjs/cloudflare](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/cloudflare.md): Why Roost wraps Cloudflare's native bindings, how typed clients improve the developer experience, and how binding names are resolved.
- [@roostjs/events](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/events.md): The observer pattern in Roost, how in-process event dispatching differs from message queues, listener vs subscriber design, and the ShouldQueue escape hatch.
- [@roostjs/feature-flags](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/feature-flags.md): Why WorkOS is the primary feature flag provider, the KV edge-cache model, the Pennant-style API design, and when to use flags vs config vs environment variables.
- [Concepts](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/index.md): Explanations of architecture, design decisions, and how things work under the hood.
- [Testing Philosophy](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/testing-philosophy.md): Why Roost uses fakes over mocks, how TestClient eliminates server setup, and what testing on Workers requires differently.
- [@roostjs/auth](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/auth.md): Why Roost delegates authentication to WorkOS, how sessions are stored on KV, and the organization model that makes multi-tenancy a first-class concern.
- [@roostjs/testing](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/testing.md): What the testing package provides, how it connects to Roost's broader testing philosophy, and the specific patterns it enables.
- [Application Architecture](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/architecture.md): How a Roost application is structured, what happens at boot time, and how a request travels from the Cloudflare edge to a response.
- [@roostjs/start](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/start.md): Why Roost integrates with TanStack Start, how the context bridge connects server and client, and what SSR looks like on Workers.
- [@roostjs/core](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/core.md): Why Roost has a service container, how the pipeline middleware model works, and what the Application class is actually doing.
- [@roostjs/broadcast](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/broadcast.md): How Durable Objects power per-channel WebSocket state, the three channel types and their authorization model, and how broadcast integrates with the events system.
- [@roostjs/workflow](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/workflow.md): How Cloudflare Workflows differ from queues, the durable execution model, saga patterns with Compensable, and when to use workflows vs jobs.
- [@roostjs/ai](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/ai.md): Why agents are classes, how the agentic loop works, and why Cloudflare Workers AI means no external API keys or network calls.
- [@roostjs/billing](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/billing.md): Why billing is an abstract interface, how the adapter pattern lets you swap providers, and the subscription lifecycle model.
- [@roostjs/orm](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/orm.md): Why Roost uses Active Record on D1, why it is not Prisma, and the design of the query builder and migration system.
- [Laravel-Inspired Patterns](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/laravel-patterns.md): Which Laravel patterns Roost adopts, what it deliberately changes, and the developer-experience philosophy behind both choices.
- [Edge Computing](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/edge-computing.md): What Cloudflare Workers are, why Roost runs on them, and how the edge runtime shapes every design decision in the framework.
- [@roostjs/schema](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/schema.md): Why Roost has a schema builder, what it produces, and why the same schema definitions power both AI tools and MCP tools.
- [@roostjs/mcp](https://roost.birdcar.dev/docs/concepts/mcp.md): What the Model Context Protocol is, why Roost supports it as a server, and how MCP tools differ from AI agent tools.
